title: Mathew Zaia
content:
People.ContactEmail mzaia@mccarthy.ca
- University of Ottawa
Mathew Zaia is an associate in the Litigation Group in Toronto, maintaining a diverse trial and appellate practice.
Before joining the Firm, Mathew clerked for Justice Suzanne Côté at the Supreme Court of Canada. Prior to that, he completed his articles at the Ontario Superior Court of Justice, where he clerked for nine judges under the supervision of Justice Renee Pomerance. Mathew also practiced at a litigation boutique and assisted clients in both trial and appellate courts, including the Court of Appeal for Ontario, in criminal and regulatory matters. His experience includes working with the Public Prosecution Service of Canada and Public Safety Canada.
Mathew obtained his Juris Doctor from the University of Ottawa, where he served as the Editor-in-Chief of the Ottawa Law Review. During law school, he was awarded the Corbin Excellence Prize in Public Law for his research on predictive technology and police powers, and he achieved top marks in several criminal law courses. His research was supervised by Justice Carissima Mathen, and he worked as a research assistant for two professors on issues of law and technology. Before law school, Mathew completed his Bachelor of Arts (Honours) and Master of Arts in Criminology at the University of Windsor, graduating at the top of his class and receiving the Board of Governors Medal in Sociology, Anthropology, and Criminology. His graduate research on surveillance and privacy earned him a SSHRC Canada Graduate Scholarship and an Ontario Graduate Scholarship.
Mathew has guest lectured at the University of Windsor on section 8 of the Charter and contributed to the editing of two books as well as Martin’s Annual Criminal Code of Canada (2024). His scholarship includes publications in several peer-reviewed journals, such as the Canadian Criminal Law Review , the Canadian Bar Review , and the Canadian Journal of Law and Technology .
Mathew was called to the Ontario bar in 2022.
